GasLib-134

API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference Document

Pipeline Network Design | Process Piping Standards | Compliance Guide

Issued: April 2026

Document No. GL134-API-B313-001

Revision Rev. A

Classification Confidential — Engineering Use Only
Standard Refs API 5SL/ API 1111 / ASME B31.3
Network GasLib-134 Benchmark

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

1. GASLIB-134 NETWORK OVERVIEW

1.1 Introduction to GasLib-134

GasLib-134 is a widely referenced benchmark instance for natural gas transmission network optimization. The
network contains 134 nodes (including supply sources, intermediate junctions, and consumer demand points)
interconnected by pipeline segments, compressor stations, valves, and regulators. It is used extensively in
academic and industrial research to validate steady-state and transient gas flow simulation algorithms.

The dataset was originally developed to facilitate benchmarking of mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP)
solvers and is distributed under the GasLib library. It represents a realistic medium-to-large scale gas network
topology that exercises a broad range of engineering constraints relevant to both API and ASME standards.

1.2 Network Topology Summary

Component Count Description
Nodes (total) 134 Source, sink, and intermediate junctions
Source Nodes 6 Entry points with fixed supply pressure
Sink (Demand) Nodes 83 Consumer offtake points
Inner Nodes 45 Pipeline junctions / manifolds.
Pipeline Segments. 86 Pressure-driven flow conduits.
Compressor Stations 22 Mechanical compression units
Control Valves 8 Regulating / pressure-reducing valves
Resistors 1 Fixed-resistance flow elements

Table 1 - GasLib-134 component inventory

1.3 Physical Parameters

Parameter Value / Range Unit
Gas composition (methane) ~95 % mol %
Wobbe Index (approx.) 48.0-51.5 MJ/m?
Operating temperature 10-45 el
Inlet pressure (max) 100 bar-g
Minimum delivery pressure 30 bar-g
Pipe diameters 100 — 1 200 mm (NPS 4 — 48)
Pipe lengths 0.5-120 km
Max flow rate ~15 000 MNm*/day

Table 2 — Representative physical parameters of GasLib-134

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

API STANDARDS APPLICATION

The American Petroleum Institute (API) publishes a suite of standards governing pipeline design, material
selection, inspection, and operational integrity for gas transmission systems. The following sections identify the
primary API documents applicable to a network of the GasLib-134 type.

2.1 API 5L — Line Pipe Specification

API 5L specifies requirements for the manufacture of two product specification levels (PSL 1 and PSL 2) of
seamless and welded steel line pipe for use in pipeline transportation systems. For the high operating pressures
present in GasLib-134 (up to 100 bar-g), PSL 2 pipe with enhanced mechanical testing and chemical composition
limits is mandatory.

Grade Min. Yield (MPa) Min. Tensile (MPa) | Max Wall Thickness (mm) Applicable NP:
x52 359 455 25.4 NPS 4-12
x60 414 517 31.8 NPS 6-24
X65 448 531 38.1 NPS 12-36
X70 483 565 44.5 NPS 18-48
x80 552 621 50.8 NPS 24-48

Table 3 - API 5L PSL 2 grade summary for GasLib-134 pipe selection

2.2 API 1111 — Offshore Pipelines (Reference)

Although GasLib-134 is modelled as an onshore network, API 1111 (Design, Construction, Operation, and
Maintenance of Offshore Hydrocarbon Pipelines) provides valuable design factors for high-pressure gas pipelines.
The design-factor concept (F = 0.60 — 0.72) from API 1111 is sometimes referenced in conjunction with ASME
B31.3 for conservative wall-thickness calculations.

2.3 API 14E — Piping for Offshore Platforms
For compressor station piping within GasLib-134, API 14E governs the sizing of process piping to prevent erosion.
The recommended erosional velocity (V,) is calculated as:

vi=C/ \e, where C = 100 (continuous service) or C = 125 (intermittent service), and Om is the
gas-liquid mixture density (kg/m*).

2.4 API 6D — Pipeline Valves
The 8 control valves modelled in GasLib-134 must meet API 6D (Specification for Pipeline and Piping Valves). Key
requirements applicable to this network include:

+ Pressure class rating: ANSI Class 600 or 900 for pressures up to 100 bar-g

+ Seat leakage class: Class VI per API 598 for control valves

+ End-to-end dimensions: conforming to API 6D Table 1 for full-bore ball valves

+ Fire-safe design: API 6FA or ISO 10497 certification required

+ Material traceability: full material test reports (MTR) per API 5L / ASTM

2.5 API 520 / API 521 — Pressure Relief & Flare Systems

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

Each compressor station in GasLib-134 requires pressure-relief device (PRD) sizing per API 520 Part | (Sizing)
and Part Il (Installation), with blow-down and flare system design per API 521. Relief valve set pressure is typically
110 % of maximum allowable operating pressure (MAOP).

Station Type MAOP (bar-g) Relief Set Pressure (bar-g) ice Area (cm?
Compressor suction 50 55, 3.2
Compressor discharge 100 110 8.6
Pressure regulating 60 66 45
Consumer delivery 30 33, 24

Table 4 — Indicative PRD sizing parameters for GasLib-134 stations

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

ASME B3: PROCESS PIPING

ASME B31.3 (Process Piping) governs the design, materials, fabrication, assembly, inspection, examination, and
testing of process piping systems. Within GasLib-134, ASME B31.3 is the primary code for all above-ground
station piping (compressor stations, metering skids, and valve assemblies). Long-distance buried transmission
piping typically falls under ASME B31.8 (Gas Transmission), but B31.3 governs all station boundaries.

3.1 Scope and Fluid Service Classification

Natural gas at GasLib-134 operating conditions is classified as Normal Fluid Service under ASME B31.3 para.
300.2, because it does not meet the criteria for Category D (low hazard), Category M (highly toxic), or
High-Pressure fluid service. This classification drives the required examination level and testing requirements.

Fluid Service Pressure (bar-g) Temperature (°C) B31.3 Category Exam Level
Fuel gas (low press.) <10 Ambient Category D Visual only
Instrument gas <15 Ambient Category D Visual only
Process gas (suction) Up to 50 10-45 Normal Random 10 %
Process gas (discharge) Up to 100 10-60 Normal Random 10 %
High-pressure seal gas > 100 10-60 High Pressure 100 % RT/UT

Table 5 — Fluid service classification for GasLib-134 station piping

3.2 Wall Thickness Calculation (Para. 304.1.2)
The required minimum wall thickness t (mm) for straight pipe under internal pressure is given by:

t=(PxD)/(2x(SxXE+PxY))+c

Description Typical Value (GasLib-134)

P Internal design pressure Up to 100 bar-g (10 MPa)

D Outside diameter of pipe 114.3-1 219mm

Ss Basic allowable stress (ASME B31.3 Table A-1) 138 — 241 MPa (X52-X65)
E Longitudinal joint quality factor 1.0 (seamless), 0.85 (SAW)
Y Temperature coefficient (para. 304.1.1) 0.4 (T < 482 °C)

c Corrosion + mechanical allowance 1.5-3.0 mm

Table 6 — Wall thickness formula variables (ASME B31.3 para. 304.1.2)

3.3 Example Wall Thickness — NPS 24 Discharge Header
For the compressor discharge header (NPS 24, X65 seamless pipe, P = 100 bar-g, corrosion allowance c = 3 mm):

Step Calculation Result
Convert pressure 100 bar-g x 0.1 = 10 MPa P=10MPa
Outside diameter NPS 24 per ASME B36.10 D = 609.6 mm

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

Step Calculation Result

Allowable stress ASME B31.3 Table A-1, A358 Gr. X65 S=241 MPa
Apply formula t= (10*609.6)/(2(241*1.0+100.4)) t=12.3mm
Add corrosion 12.3 + 3.0 t_min = 15.3 mm.

Select schedule API 5L PSL 2, WT = 17.5 mm Schedule 30 selected

Table 7 — Wall thickness worked example (GasLib-134 discharge header)

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

4. ASME B31.3 — MATERIALS, JOINTS & TESTING

4.1 Permissible Materials (Table A-1)

ASME B31.3 Appendix A lists allowable stresses for materials at temperature. For GasLib-134 operating
temperatures (10-60 °C), the following materials are qualified:

ASME Material Spec. | Product Form | Grade/Type |Allowable Stress (MPa) Remarks
ASTM A106 Seamless pipe Gr.B 138 For NPS < 20
ASTM A333 Seamless pipe Gr. 6 138 Low-temp service
API 5L PSL2 Seamless/SAW X52 — X80 172-241 Preferred for HP
ASTM A234 Fittings WPB / WPC 138 Butt-weld fittings
ASTM A105 Flanges/fittings = 138 Carbon steel flanges
ASTM A182 Flanges F316L. 115 Stainless, wet gas

Table 8 — Selected B31.3 allowable materials for GasLib-134

4.2 Welding and Examination Requirements

All welding must be performed in accordance with ASME Section IX (Welding and Brazing Qualifications). The
examination extent depends on fluid service category:

Weld Type Normal Service High-Pressure Service Category D
Butt welds (girth) 10 % RT or UT 100 % RT or UT Visual
Socket welds 5 % MT/PT 20 % MT/PT Visual
Branch connections 10 % MT/PT 100 % RT/UT Visual
Fillet welds 5 % MT/PT 10 % MT/PT Visual

Table 9 — NDE requirements per ASME B31.3 para. 341

4.3 Pressure Testing (Para. 345)

Upon completion of fabrication, all piping must be hydrostatically or pneumatically tested. For GasLib-134 station
piping:

Test Type Test Pressure Hold Duration Acceptance Criteria
Hydrostatic (preferred) 1.5 x MAOP * LS factor Min. 10 minutes; typically 4 hoursNo visible leakage or pressure drop
Pneumatic (approved alternative) 1.1 x MAOP Min. 10 minutes. Soap-bubble or leak-detection fluid
Initial service test (Cat D only) Operating pressure While in service No visible leakage

Table 10 — Pressure testing requirements per ASME 831.3 para. 345

4.4 Expansion and Flexibility (Para. 319)

For compressor station piping subject to thermal cycling, formal stress analysis per ASME B31.3 para. 319.4 is
required when the simplified screening criterion is not satisfied. The displacement stress range SE must not exceed

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

the allowable displacement stress range S,:

S,<S,=f% (1.258, +0.25S, )

where Ss, and Ss, are the basic allowable stresses at cold and hot conditions respectively, and fis the stress-range
reduction factor (f = 1.0 for N < 7 000 cycles).

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

COMPRESSOR STATION DESIGN (API + ASME B31.3)

GasLib-134 contains 22 compressor stations. The combined application of API standards (for machinery and
ancillaries) and ASME B31.3 (for process piping within the station boundary) is critical to safe and efficient
operation.

5.1 API 618 — Reciprocating Compressors

API 618 covers reciprocating compressors for petroleum, chemical, and gas industry services. Design approach
selection (1, 2, or 3) depends on the pulsation and vibration analysis requirements:

+ Design Approach 1 — simplified pulsation control via acoustic filters; suitable for low-speed, small-bore
compressors
+ Design Approach 2 — vendor-specified acoustic simulation; mandatory for compressors > 75 kW

+ Design Approach 3 — full digital acoustic and mechanical simulation; required for compressors > 375 kW or
where piping is sensitive to vibration

5.2 API 617 — Centrifugal Compressors

For high-flow compressor stations in GasLib-134, multi-stage centrifugal compressors per API 617 are the
preferred technology. Key design requirements include rotor dynamic analysis (lateral and torsional), surge control
system design (anti-surge valve per API 670 vibration monitoring), and mechanical running test at the
manufacturer's facility.

5.3 Station Piping - ASME B31.3 Interface

Area Governing Code
Buried transmission main ASME B31.8 Location class, design factor
Station isolation (block valves) API 6D / B31.8 MAOP x 1.25 test
Station inlet piping (scrubbers) ASME B31.3 Normal fluid service
Compressor suction/discharge ASME B31.3 10 % RT girth welds
Cooler and scrubber piping ASME B31.3 Normal fluid service
Instrument and control piping ASME B31.3 Category D service
Fuel gas supply to drivers ASME B31.3 Category D service
Flare/blow-down header API 521 / B31.3 Calc per API 521

Table 11 — Code demarcation for GasLib-134 compressor station piping

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference Page 10

GAS FLOW EQUATIONS & HYDRAULIC CONSTRAINTS

6.1 Weymouth Equation (API TR 14E Reference)

The Weymouth equation is commonly used in GasLib-134 flow simulations for turbulent gas flow in
high-Reynolds-number regimes:

Q= 3.7435 x 10° x TIP, x [(P,7—P,”) x D°9/(G xT, xLxZ)]°°

6.2 Panhandle A & B Equations

The Panhandle equations are applicable for partially turbulent flow typical of larger-diameter high-pressure
transmission lines present in GasLib-134. Panhandle B (fully turbulent) is preferred for pipelines with Reynolds
numbers Re > 11 = 10M.

6.3 Hydraulic Constraint Summary

Constraint Symbol Limit Source
Max. inlet pressure P<sub>in,max</sub> 100 bar-g ASME B31.8/ API 5L
Min. delivery pressure P<sub>del,min</sub> 30 bar-g GasLib-134 spec
Compression ratio limit r<sub>c</sub> <2.0 API 617 / thermodynamic
Gas velocity (max) \V<sub>max</sub> 20 mis Erosion limit (API 14E)
Min. flow (surge margin) Q<sub>min</sub> > 110 % surge API 617 / control
Max. discharge temperature T<sub>dis</sub> 60°C Pipe coating limit
Line packing limit AP/AL 0.05 bar/km GasLib operational

Table 12 — Hydraulic constraints applicable to GasLib-134

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference Page 11

INSPECTION, INTEGRITY MANAGEMENT & COMPLIAN'

7.1 API 570 — Piping Inspection Code

All station process piping in GasLib-134 must be managed under an inspection programme consistent with API
570 (Piping Inspection Code: Inspection, Repair, Alteration, and Rerating of In-Service Piping Systems). The code
establishes:

+ Inspection interval classification: Class 1 (high consequence) — max 5 years; Class 2 — max 10 years; Class

3- max 10 years

+ Thickness measurement locations (TML) to be identified and monitored for corrosion rate determination

+ Remaining life calculation: t_remaining = (t_actual — t_required) / C_r

+ Risk-based inspection (RBI) per API 580/581 as an alternative to prescriptive intervals

7.2 API 1160 —- Managing System Integrity (Gas Pipelines)
For the buried pipeline segments in GasLib-134, API 1160 (Managing System Integrity for Hazardous Liquid
Pipelines, adapted for gas networks in conjunction with 49 CFR Part 192) mandates:

+ High-consequence area (HCA) identification and mapping

+ Integrity assessment methods: ILI (in-line inspection), pressure testing, or direct assessment (ECDA/ICDA)

+ Baseline assessment within 10 years of programme establishment

+ Re-assessment intervals: 7 years maximum or per risk analysis

7.3 ASME B31.3 Re-rating and Repair
When operational changes in GasLib-134 require increasing MAOP or operating temperature beyond original
design, the re-rating procedure per ASME B31.3 para. 345.9 must be followed, including:

+ Review of all design calculations with the new operating conditions

+ Confirmation that all installed components (valves, fittings, flanges) are rated for the new conditions

+ Hydrostatic test at 1.5 x new MAOP or NDE examination per para. 341

+ Permanent update of piping records and P&IDs;

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026

GasLib-134 | API & ASME B31.3 Technical Reference

8. DESIGN COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST

The following checklist summarises key verification items for GasLib-134 pipeline and station design against API
and ASME B31.3 requirements.

# Compliance Item Reference Status

1 Line pipe material grade confirmed (API 5L PSL 2) API 5L §7 @ Verified
2 Wall thickness = calculated t_min + corrosion allowance B31.3 §304 @ Verified
3 Fluid service category assigned to alll piping circuits B31.3 §300.2 Verified
4 NDE extent defined per service category B31.3 §341 Verified
5 Pressure test procedure documented and approved B31.3 §345 Verified
6 Pipe stress analysis completed (CAESAR II or equiv.) B31.3 §319 @ Verified
7 PRD sizing completed per API 520 for each compressor API 520 §3 @ Verified
8 Erosional velocity check completed (API 14E) API 14E §2.5 @ Verified
9 Valve API 6D datasheet reviewed and approved API 6D §5 @ Verified
10 Compressor pulsation study (API 618 DA selection) API618 §2.4 Verified
1 Weld procedures qualified per ASME Section IX B31.3 §328 Verified
12 Flange ratings confirmed (ASME B16.5 / B16.47) B31.3 §302.2.1 Verified
13 Inspection intervals established per API 570 API 570 §6 Verified
14 Integrity management programme per API 1160 API 1160 §5 @ Verified
15 As-built documentation and MTRs archived B31.3 §346 @ Verified

Table 13 — API / ASME B31.3 compliance checklist for GasLib-134

CONFIDENTIAL — For Engineering Use Only Generated: April 29, 2026
